20 | In answer to the unverified First Amended Complaint (“Complaint”) on file herein,
21|| defendant UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, HASTINGS COLLEGE OF THE LAW (sued herein
22||as HASTINGS COLLEGE OF THE LAW) answers plaintiff LAWRENCE LESS as follows:
23 Responding defendant denies, generally and specifically, each and every, all and singular,
24 || the allegations of plaintiff's Complaint. Further responding to plaintiff's Complaint, defendant
25 || denies that plaintiff was injured in any manner, or at all.
26 Further responding to plaintiffs Complaint, defendant asserts each of the following
27 || separate and independent affirmative defenses:
28||///
-1-
DEFENDANT UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, HASTINGS COLLEGE OF THE LAW’S
ANSWER TO PLAINTIFF’S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

 
1 FIRST AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE
2 Failure to State a Claim
3 The Complaint, and each and every cause of action therein, fail to state facts sufficient to
4 constitute a cause of action against this responding defendant.
5 SECOND AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE
6 Comparative Negligence
7 Any injury or damage sustained by plaintiff was the result of plaintiffs own intentional,
8 | negligent or otherwise wrongful acts, and any claim against this responding defendant should be |
9 reduced in proportion to the fault of the plaintiff.

10 THIRD AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE

11 Fault of Third Parties

12 Any injury or damage to plaintiff was a result of the intentional, negligent or otherwise

13] wrongful acts of third parties, and any claims against this responding defendant should be reduced

14] in proportion to the fault of those third parties. Under the principles formulated in the case of

15 || American Motorcycle Association v. Superior Court (1978) 20 Cal.3d 578, this defendant prays

16] that the percentage of such contribution be established by special verdict or other procedure.

17 FOURTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE

18 Obvious Danger

19 If there were a danger or defect, which there was not, it was an open and obvious condition,

20 || such that plaintiff should have avoided the hazard, if any, in the exercise of due care.

21 FIFTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE

22 Equitable Defenses

23 Plaintiff is precluded from seeking and recovering the relief prayed for, in whole or in part,

24 I by operation of the doctrines of consent, bar and merger, unclean hands, and/or laches.

25 SIXTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE

26 Limitation of Liability

27 Any injuries or damages claimed by plaintiff were caused or contributed to by the negligent

28}; acts or omissions of persons, firms and entities, including public entities, other than this

-2-
DEFENDANT UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, HASTINGS COLLEGE OF THE LAW’S
ANSWER TO PLAINTIFF’S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

 
1 | responding defendant, so that plaintiff's recovery for any physical injury or property damage, as |
2 || well as non-economic damages, should be reduced on the basis of comparative fault pursuant to |
3 || Civil Code § 1431.2. |
4 SEVENTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE |
5 Failure to Mitigate
6 Plaintiff failed to take reasonable steps to mitigate his damages, so that plaintiffs recovery, |
7|\if any, should be correspondingly diminished.
8 . EIGHTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE
9 Assumption of the Risk |
10 Plaintiff knew, or in the exercise of reasonable care should have known, of the risks and
11]|hazards involved in the undertaking in which he engaged, but nevertheless and with full |
12}| knowledge of these things, did fully and voluntarily consent to assume the risks and hazards
13 |} involved in the undertaking. Plaintiff also expressly assumed the risk of injury by entering the |
14|| premises where the accident allegedly occurred, and the loss or damage, if any, sustained by |
15]| plaintiff was caused by the risks that were accepted and voluntarily assumed by plaintiff when
16 || engaging in such activity. :
17 NINTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE |
18 Lack of Notice |
19 This answering defendant alleges it was without actual or constructive notice of the defect |
20 || or condition of which plaintiff complains, that the condition of property complained of did not |
21 || create a substantial risk of injury to foreseeable users who exercised due care, and that the cost of |
22 || operating and maintaining an inspection system that would have been adequate to discover and |
23 || cure the alleged defect or condition of which plaintiff complains would have been unreasonably
24 || expensive and impractical in relation to the risk involved. |
25 TENTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE |
26 Statute of Limitations |
27 This answering defendant alleges that each and every allegation contained in plaintiffs |
28 || Complaint is barred by the statutes of limitation as set forth in the California Government Code as |
-3- |
DEFENDANT UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, HASTINGS COLLEGE OF THE LAW’S
ANSWER TO PLAINTIFF’S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

 
1 well as Code of Civil Procedure § 335 and continuing through § 349.4, including, but not limited
2|| to, the following: C.C.P. §§ 335, 335.1, 337, 337.1, 337.15, 338, 339, 339.5, 340, 342, and 343.
3 ELEVENTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE
4 Knowledge of Condition .
5 This answering defendant alleges that plaintiff had full and complete knowledge of any
6 || alleged defective or dangerous condition which existed in or upon the property mentioned in
7\| plaintiff's Complaint, and with full and sufficient knowledge of said alleged dangerous or
8 || defective condition plaintiff assumed any such risk to plaintiffs safety involving the use of said
9 || property.
10 TWELFTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE
ll Waiver and Estoppel
12 This answering defendant alleges that by reason of plaintiff's own acts and omissions,
13 || plaintiff has waived and is estopped from seeking any recovery from defendant by reason of the
14 || allegations set forth in the Complaint.
15 THIRTEENTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE
16 No Duty & No Causation
17 This answering defendant alleges that as a matter of law, this answering defendant owed no
18 || legal duty to plaintiff and defendant’s conduct was not the legal, proximate, or other cause of
19]| plaintiff's alleged injury or damages, if any there were.
20 FOURTEENTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE
21 Trivial Defect
22 This answering defendant alleges that as a matter of law, the risk created by the subject
23 || condition was of such a minor, trivial and insignificant nature in view of the surrounding
24 || circumstances that no reasonable person could conclude that the situation created a substantial risk
25|| of injury when the subject property was used with due care and in a manner in which it was
26 || reasonably foreseeable that it would be used.
27\\///
28 |) ///
-4.
DEFENDANT UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, HASTINGS COLLEGE OF THE LAW’S
ANSWER TO PLAINTIFF’S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

 
1 FIFTEENTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE
2 Product & Premises Misuse
3 This answering defendant alleges that as a matter of law, the manner of plaintiffs use of
4 the subject product and premises was unforeseeable, inappropriate, and improper and constituted a
5 || misuse of said product and premises, completely barring and/or proportionately reducing recovery
6 | herein.
7 SIXTEENTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE
8 Governmental Immunities
9 Plaintiff's Complaint and each of its purported causes of action are barred by governmental
10 fl immunities, including but not limited to those set forth in Government Code §§ 815.2, 818.8,
11 }} 820.2, 820.4, 820.6, 822.2, 830.6, 831 and any other immunity that defendant determines may
12 | apply after discovery of the factual basis for plaintiffs claims.
13 SEVENTEENTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE
14 Failure to Exhaust Administrative Remedies
15 The Complaint and each of its purported causes of action are barred by plaintiff's failure to
16 || exhaust available administrative remedies, including but not limited to those provided under
17 | Government Code sections 900 et seq.
18 | EIGHTEENTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE
19 | Additional Defenses Reserved
20] This answering defendant hereby specifically reserves the right to add and allege such
21 | additional affirmative defenses as are not presently known to defendant but which may become
22 || reasonably apparent or manifest as discovery proceeds in this action and additional facts are
23 | discovered and become known.
24
25 WHEREFORE, defendant prays for judgment as follows:
26 1. That plaintiff takes nothing from defendant;
27 2. That the Complaint be dismissed with prejudice;
28 3. That defendant recovers costs of suit herein, including attorneys’ fees; and
-5-
DEFENDANT UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, HASTINGS COLLEGE OF THE LAW’S
ANSWER TO PLAINTIFE’S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

 
1 4. For such other relief as is just and proper.
